name: dhdna-profiler
tagline: Scores any text on 12 'cognitive DNA' dimensions to build a narrative psychological/thinking-style profile of its author.
verdict: No security red flags: no secrets, network calls, shell execution, or destructive actions — it's a pure Read/analyze/present text-profiling framework. Minor gaps: declares an unused 'Write' tool, gives no guidance against profiling third parties without consent or handling thin/ambiguous text, and lacks a worked example, but nothing here is unsafe to install

highlights.summary: No explicit 'do not use for X' guidance — e.g. no caution against profiling a third party's writing without their knowledge/consent, or using the output for hiring/vetting decisions; 'What This Is NOT

No instructions for edge cases: very short/thin text, non-English text, or text with insufficient signal beyond tagging confidence as LOW — no guidance to caveat or decline when evidence is too sparse
Output format (lines 101-131) is a template with placeholder text only ('9/10', bracketed fields) — no fully worked example profile is shown to anchor how ambiguous scoring judgments should resolve.
